title: Medical cannulae, delivery systems and methods
abstract: The disclosure relates to cannulae, delivery systems, methods of making cannulae, and methods of making delivery systems. A delivery system comprises an elongate outer tubular member defining an outer tubular member lumen, a cannula having a circumferential wall extending between a proximal end and a distal end and defining an interior lumen, and an intraluminal medical device disposed within the outer tubular member lumen distal to the cannula and not about the cannula. A pattern of openings arranged in an interrupted spiral extends circumferentially along the cannula.
claims: 1. A medical cannula, comprising: an elongate tubular member having a lengthwise axis and a circumferential wall extending between a proximal end and a distal end and defining an interior lumen, the circumferential wall having an outer circumferential surface defining a pattern of openings comprising a plurality of openings arranged in an interrupted spiral that extends circumferentially along the elongate tubular member, each opening of the plurality of openings having a first end that is curved, a first portion of the circumferential wall disposed between immediately adjacent openings within a first revolution of the interrupted spiral, a second portion of the circumferential wall disposed between immediately adjacent openings within a second revolution of the interrupted spiral, the first portion having a first circumferential length along the elongate tubular member, the second portion having a second circumferential length along the elongate tubular member that is different than the first circumferential length; and an outer member disposed circumferentially about the elongate tubular member; wherein a third portion of the circumferential wall is disposed between a first pair of immediately adjacent revolutions of the interrupted spiral, the third portion having a first axial length along the lengthwise axis of the elongate tubular member; and wherein a fourth portion of the circumferential wall is disposed between a second pair of immediately adjacent revolutions of the interrupted spiral, the fourth portion having a second axial length along the lengthwise axis of the elongate tubular member that is different than the first axial length.
